/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import os, json
for p in ['kg/graph.json','kg/GRAPH_REPORT.md','kg/KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH.md']:
    print('\\nFILE',p)
    with open(p,'r',encoding='utf-8',errors='ignore') as f:
        txt=f.read(4000)
    print(txt[:4000])
PY"

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
from openpyxl import Workbook
from openpyxl.chart import BarChart, Reference
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter
from pathlib import Path

out = Path('model_output/financial-table-key-expense-analysis-concise-version.xlsx')
wb = Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Expense Comparison'

# explanatory note
notes = [
    'Data availability note:',
    'No accessible source files matching the requested financial report dashboard, income/expense visualization charts, or profit statement were present in the working directory.',
    'Semantic search returned no results, and directory inspection found no usable financial source files.',
    'Therefore, the Top 5 expense items by amount could not be extracted without fabricating data.'
]
for i, t in enumerate(notes, start=1):
    ws.cell(row=i, column=1, value=t)
    if i == 1:
        ws.cell(row=i, column=1).font = Font(bold=True)

start = 7
headers = ['Source File', 'Rank', 'Expense Item', 'Amount', 'Status / Error']
for c, h in enumerate(headers, start=1):
    cell = ws.cell(row=start, column=c, value=h)
    cell.font = Font(bold=True, color='FFFFFF')
    cell.fill = PatternFill('solid', fgColor='4F81BD')
    cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal='center')

rows = [
    ['Financial report dashboard', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
    ['Income and expense visualization charts', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
    ['Profit statement', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
]
for r_idx, row in enumerate(rows, start=start+1):
    for c_idx, val in enumerate(row, start=1):
        ws.cell(row=r_idx, column=c_idx, value=val)

# widths
widths = {1:30,2:10,3:22,4:14,5:80}
for col_idx, width in widths.items():
    ws.column_dimensions[get_column_letter(col_idx)].width = width

# chart data section
chart_row = start + len(rows) + 4
ws.cell(row=chart_row, column=1, value='Chart placeholder data')
ws.cell(row=chart_row+1, column=1, value='Category')
ws.cell(row=chart_row+1, column=2, value='Amount')
for i, cat in enumerate(['Dashboard','Income/Expense Charts','Profit Statement'], start=chart_row+2):
    ws.cell(row=i, column=1, value=cat)
    ws.cell(row=i, column=2, value=0)

chart = BarChart()
chart.type = 'bar'
chart.style = 10
chart.title = 'High-expense items comparison (unavailable source data shown as 0)'
chart.y_axis.title = 'Source'
chart.x_axis.title = 'Amount'

data = Reference(ws, min_col=2, min_row=chart_row+1, max_row=chart_row+4)
cats = Reference(ws, min_col=1, min_row=chart_row+2, max_row=chart_row+4)
chart.add_data(data, titles_from_data=True)
chart.set_categories(cats)
chart.height = 7
chart.width = 14
ws.add_chart(chart, 'G7')

wb.save(out)
print(out)
PY"

/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
from openpyxl import Workbook
from openpyxl.chart import BarChart, Reference
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment
from openpyxl.utils import get_column_letter
from pathlib import Path

out = Path('model_output/financial-table-key-expense-analysis-concise-version.xlsx')
wb = Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Expense Comparison'

# explanatory note
notes = [
    'Data availability note:',
    'No accessible source files matching the requested financial report dashboard, income/expense visualization charts, or profit statement were present in the working directory.',
    'Semantic search returned no results, and directory inspection found no usable financial source files.',
    'Therefore, the Top 5 expense items by amount could not be extracted without fabricating data.'
]
for i, t in enumerate(notes, start=1):
    ws.cell(row=i, column=1, value=t)
    if i == 1:
        ws.cell(row=i, column=1).font = Font(bold=True)

start = 7
headers = ['Source File', 'Rank', 'Expense Item', 'Amount', 'Status / Error']
for c, h in enumerate(headers, start=1):
    cell = ws.cell(row=start, column=c, value=h)
    cell.font = Font(bold=True, color='FFFFFF')
    cell.fill = PatternFill('solid', fgColor='4F81BD')
    cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal='center')

rows = [
    ['Financial report dashboard', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
    ['Income and expense visualization charts', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
    ['Profit statement', 1, None, None, 'SOURCE INACCESSIBLE: no matching file found in working directory / semantic search no results'],
]
for r_idx, row in enumerate(rows, start=start+1):
    for c_idx, val in enumerate(row, start=1):
        ws.cell(row=r_idx, column=c_idx, value=val)

# widths
widths = {1:30,2:10,3:22,4:14,5:80}
for col_idx, width in widths.items():
    ws.column_dimensions[get_column_letter(col_idx)].width = width

# chart data section
chart_row = start + len(rows) + 4
ws.cell(row=chart_row, column=1, value='Chart placeholder data')
ws.cell(row=chart_row+1, column=1, value='Category')
ws.cell(row=chart_row+1, column=2, value='Amount')
for i, cat in enumerate(['Dashboard','Income/Expense Charts','Profit Statement'], start=chart_row+2):
    ws.cell(row=i, column=1, value=cat)
    ws.cell(row=i, column=2, value=0)

chart = BarChart()
chart.type = 'bar'
chart.style = 10
chart.title = 'High-expense items comparison (unavailable source data shown as 0)'
chart.y_axis.title = 'Source'
chart.x_axis.title = 'Amount'

data = Reference(ws, min_col=2, min_row=chart_row+1, max_row=chart_row+4)
cats = Reference(ws, min_col=1, min_row=chart_row+2, max_row=chart_row+4)
chart.add_data(data, titles_from_data=True)
chart.set_categories(cats)
chart.height = 7
chart.width = 14
ws.add_chart(chart, 'G7')

wb.save(out)
print(out)
PY"
